raw-backfill: incidents ingest. The select_raw_batch.py digest above lists unprocessed raw files. Your FIRST response MUST be a tool call (file_read on the first raw file, or $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md for the workflow). For each file, run the §Ingest workflow in $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md: create a scored source page keyed by `raw:`; extract entities worth tracking; cross-link, tagging any recurring cross-cutting theme as `[[concepts/<slug>]]` even before the concept page exists (the signal concept-backfill needs). File a prediction only for an explicit, falsifiable, dated claim. WRITE via the MCP write path (mcp_okengine_write_create_entity / update_entity), NOT file_write; never write findings/ (human-only). LOCAL-ONLY: do not call web_search/web_extract/web_crawl (shared paid budget). Append one wiki/log.md summary line.
entity-backfill: incidents entity backfill. The select_entity_candidates.py digest above lists existing entity pages + recent sources. FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md, create or update entity pages for subjects worth tracking over time; dedupe against existing; cross-link — and where an entity exhibits a recurring cross-cutting theme, tag it `[[concepts/<slug>]]` even if that concept page doesn't exist yet (the dangling link is the signal concept-backfill needs; do NOT create concept pages here). WRITE via the MCP write path; for LIST fields like `sources:` send the COMPLETE updated list (read first, append, send all). Do NOT create concepts or predictions here. LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
concept-backfill: incidents concept backfill. The select_concept_candidates.py digest above lists missing concept slugs (wikilinked but no page). FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md, create concept pages (a cross-cutting pattern / segment / cluster) synthesized from the citing sources. If a slug is really an entity name, it is an ENTITY not a concept — skip and note it. WRITE via mcp_okengine_write_create_entity. LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
source-quality-backfill: Source scoring. The select_unscored_sources.py digest above lists source pages lacking your quality fields. FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per the rubric in $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md, score each by UPDATING FRONTMATTER ONLY via mcp_okengine_write_update_entity (pass frontmatter_yaml, NO body — the body is preserved). LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
schema-classify-drain: Type classification. The select_schema_classify.py digest above lists entities typed `entity`/`organization` that need a canonical type. FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per the $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md taxonomy, set `type:` to the single best canonical type via mcp_okengine_write_update_entity (frontmatter merge) — or leave the page alone if no canonical type honestly fits. LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
page-quality-enrich: Deepen thin pages. The select_page_quality_enrich.py digest above lists deficient entity/concept pages with the source pages that cite each. FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md, AUGMENT each target — append 2-4 `##` sections of real analysis grounded ONLY in the citing sources — via mcp_okengine_write_append_to_section. Never replace existing content. LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
prediction-grade: Resolve overdue predictions. The select_predictions_for_grading.py digest above lists open predictions whose `resolves_by` has passed. FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md, for each: ADD a `## Postmortem` via mcp_okengine_write_append_to_section, then flip `status:` (confirmed/refuted/partial/expired-ungraded) via mcp_okengine_write_update_entity. Never edit made_on/horizon/basis. LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
prediction-regrade: Re-grade open predictions against new evidence. The select_regrade_batch.py digest above lists open predictions + new source pages. FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md, for each source that bears on a claim, append a structured record to the body `## Evidence log` (append_to_section) and update the `evidence:` list + top-level `confidence:` via update_entity (send the COMPLETE evidence list). No-op is correct when no new source bears. LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
prediction-candidate-watch: File prediction candidates. The select_prediction_candidates.py digest above lists entities with recent activity and no open prediction. FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md, FILE a falsifiable, dated prediction (mcp_okengine_write_create_entity in predictions/, including a `## What would refute this` section) ONLY when a specific, observable, dated claim is genuinely warranted; DEFER otherwise — conservative bias, coverage is not a goal. LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
trends-refresh: incidents trend synthesis. The select_trend_deltas.py digest above lists entities whose recent source-mention frequency rose sharply over the rolling window. Synthesize 1-3 grounded `trend` pages at wiki/trends/<slug> (frontmatter: type: trend, title, period: <e.g. '2026-05..2026-06'>, direction: <up|down|shift>, updated: <YYYY-MM-DD using TODAY's date>) — each a DATED, DIRECTIONAL observation over the window (NOT a timeless concept, NOT a daily snapshot), with [[wikilinks]] to the driving entities + their recent sources and a one-line 'why it's moving'. Ground each trend in the underlying entities first (retrieve_context / search on the okengine read MCP). Write via the MCP write path; skip a candidate that is noise rather than a real directional shift. LOCAL-ONLY: do not use web tools. End your response with a one-line summary of the trends written.
daily-brief:
Daily incidents brief. Your FIRST response MUST be a tool call: file_read on wiki/HOT.md (the derived load-first set: recent sources, recently-updated entities, open predictions). Then write today's brief to wiki/briefings/<YYYY-MM-DD>.md (frontmatter: type: briefing, title, published: <YYYY-MM-DD>, updated: <YYYY-MM-DD> — use TODAY's actual date, not a guessed or future one, for the filename and both date fields; published drives tier recency) summarizing the 3-5 most significant developments, each 2-3 sentences with [[wikilinks]] to the entities + their source. Expert-grade and terse; skip 101 explanation. If the vault is essentially empty, say so in one line instead of padding. LOCAL-ONLY: do not use web tools.

The select_daily_brief.py digest above lists the window's fresh sources, entity movement, due predictions, and new gaps — TRUST THE DIGEST; do not tour the vault.
